analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 10 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: SoC control APB registers directly update boot, fetch, cluster power/reset/fetch/IRQ, AXI-route, JTAG, pad mux/config state on raw APB write decode without any visible privilege, lifecycle, debug-authorization, lock, or post-boot guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger / fc_bootaddr_o/r_bootaddr, fc_fetchen_o/r_fetchen, cluster_boot_addr_o/r_cluster_boot, cluster_fetch_enable_o/r_cluster_fetch_enable, cluster_rstn_o/r_cluster_rstn, cluster_pow_o/r_cluster_pow, cluster_byp_o/r_cluster_byp, sel_hyper_axi_o/r_sel_hyper_axi, cluster_irq_o/r_cluster_irq, soc_jtag_reg_o/r_jtag_rego, pad mux/config registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester reaching soc_peripherals/s_soc_ctrl_bus through periph_bus_wrap Operation: write/configure/trigger Object: fc_bootaddr_o/r_bootaddr, fc_fetchen_o/r_fetchen, cluster_boot_addr_o/r_cluster_boot, cluster_fetch_enable_o/r_cluster_fetch_enable, cluster_rstn_o/r_cluster_rstn, cluster_pow_o/r_cluster_pow, cluster_byp_o/r_cluster_byp, sel_hyper_axi_o/r_sel_hyper_axi, cluster_irq_o/r_cluster_irq, soc_jtag_reg_o/r_jtag_rego, pad mux/config registers Expected guard: Writes to SoC control registers that change boot address, fetch enable, cluster reset/power/bypass, IRQ, and AXI route selection should be mediated by a privilege/lifecycle/debug-authorization or equivalent access-control condition beyond raw APB decode. Observed behavior: The apb_soc_ctrl write process enters the register write case solely under PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. REG_FCBOOT assigns r_bootaddr <= PWDATA; REG_FCFETCH assigns r_fetchen <= PWDATA[0]; REG_CLUSTER_CTRL assigns cluster bypass/power/fetch_enable/rstn from PWDATA[3:0]; REG_CTRL_PER assigns r_sel_hyper_axi from PWDATA[0] under HYPER_RAM; REG_CLUSTER_IRQ assigns r_cluster_irq; REG_CLUSTER_BOOT_ADDR0/1 assign r_cluster_boot. No guard, lock, privilege, lifecycle, or 'fetch already enabled' condition is visible at the write sites. Path: pulp_soc APB/peripheral interconnect -> soc_peripherals periph_bus_i -> s_soc_ctrl_bus -> apb_soc_ctrl APB register case decode Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An APB master that reaches the SoC-control address range can alter execution start/fetch behavior, cluster reset/power/fetch state, trigger cluster IRQ, change cluster boot address, and potentially change AXI route/pad/JTAG-related outputs, enabling control-flow or availability compromise of SoC/cluster operation.

findings.summary: The JTAG lint wrapper connects external JTAG controls directly to the internal lint_debug bus used by the SoC interconnect, and no debug authentication or lifecycle enable is visible at that entry point.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/access/route / L2/SCM/ROM/peripheral spaces reachable through soc_interconnect_wrap lint_debug path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug requester Operation: read/write/access/route Object: L2/SCM/ROM/peripheral spaces reachable through soc_interconnect_wrap lint_debug path Expected guard: Debug/JTAG-originated memory or peripheral accesses should be authorized by debug authentication/lifecycle enable and should preserve address-range and access-policy checks for protected memories/peripherals. Observed behavior: pulp_soc instantiates soc_interconnect_wrap with lint_debug connected to s_lint_debug_bus and instantiates lint_jtag_wrap with JTAG TAP-like signals and jtag_lint_master connected to the same s_lint_debug_bus. The visible connection includes selection/reset/clock signals but no authentication/lifecycle/debug-enable guard. Path: jtag_* top-level inputs -> lint_jtag_wrap i_lint_jtag -> s_lint_debug_bus -> soc_interconnect_wrap lint_debug -> memory/peripheral interconnect targets Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If lint_debug is routed to protected memories/peripherals by the interconnect, an unauthenticated JTAG path could read or write internal memory/peripheral resources while bypassing CPU-originated policy. The exact target range and denial behavior require the full interconnect decode.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full soc_interconnect decode and lint_jtag_wrap implementation are not provided in the observations, so exact protected target reachability and unauthorized-state response are not fully proven.

findings.summary: FLL lock inputs are synchronized and exposed as readable status, but the APB write decode and FLL request/write data paths do not gate write access with fll*_lock_sync.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/read / fll1_req/fll1_wrn/fll1_add/fll1_data, fll2_*, fll3_* frequency/clock configuration interface / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester reaching FLL interface Operation: write/configure/read Object: fll1_req/fll1_wrn/fll1_add/fll1_data, fll2_*, fll3_* frequency/clock configuration interface Expected guard: Writes/configuration updates to FLL control/status registers should be blocked or constrained when corresponding fll*_lock indicates locked state. Observed behavior: For PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE, PADDR[5:2] selects FLL1/FLL2/FLL3 direct access and sets fll*_wr_access with write_ready=rvalid. The write data/address/write-not-read outputs are driven from fll*_valid, PWRITE, PADDR, and PWDATA. The only observed use of fll*_lock_sync is in readback at address 4'b1111. Path: APB bus -> s_fll_bus -> apb_fll_if -> soc_fll_master/soc_fll_slave/cluster_fll_master FLL ports Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An APB master can attempt to reconfigure clock/FLL settings even while the corresponding FLL reports locked, potentially perturbing frequency/clock behavior of SoC domains and bypassing the intended lock semantics.

findings.summary: The APB peripheral address map contains overlapping ranges, while apb_node generates PSEL independently for every matching inclusive range, creating alias/multiple-select exposure for protected peripherals.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/read/write / APB slave select signals for GPIO, uDMA, soc_ctrl, adv_timer, event generator, EU, and protected configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Any APB master on peripheral bus Operation: route/read/write Object: APB slave select signals for GPIO, uDMA, soc_ctrl, adv_timer, event generator, EU, and protected configuration registers Expected guard: Peripheral address decoding should enforce non-overlapping ranges, generate PSEL only for intended slaves, and return errors/no access for unmapped or out-of-range addresses without aliasing protected registers. Observed behavior: apb_node assigns psel_o[i] = (paddr_i >= START_ADDR_i[i]) && (paddr_i <= END_ADDR_i[i]) for every master independently. periph_bus_wrap populates START/END constants from periph_bus_defines. Because the constants overlap, a single address can assert multiple PSELs, including GPIO plus SOC_CTRL/UDMA for 0x1A10_4000-0x1A10_4FFF. Path: APB bus -> periph_bus_wrap -> apb_node_wrap/apb_node -> s_masters[*] PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE propagation to individual APB slaves Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Address aliasing or multiple slave selection can expose protected control registers through unintended regions, cause unintended simultaneous writes to multiple peripherals, and make PRDATA/PREADY/PSLVERR mux behavior ambiguous for protected APB transactions.

findings.summary: soc_peripherals instantiates udma_subsystem with APB PADDR/PWDATA/PWRITE/PSEL/PENABLE from s_udma_bus and L2 read-only/write-only master ports l2_tx_master and l2_rx_master. The module body containing channel address/length/control and any range checks was not provided.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMA read/write/configure / L2 memory bus paths reachable by uDMA TX/RX L2_ro/L2_wo ports / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: uDMA TX/RX engine Operation: DMA read/write/configure Object: L2 memory bus paths reachable by uDMA TX/RX L2_ro/L2_wo ports Expected guard: uDMA-generated read/write transfers should be constrained by address range checks or configuration permissions, and configuration updates should not alter active transfer addresses/lengths to bypass intended bounds. Observed behavior: soc_peripherals instantiates udma_subsystem with APB PADDR/PWDATA/PWRITE/PSEL/PENABLE from s_udma_bus and L2 read-only/write-only master ports l2_tx_master and l2_rx_master. The module body containing channel address/length/control and any range checks was not provided. Path: APB s_udma_bus config -> udma_subsystem -> l2_tx_master/l2_rx_master -> soc_interconnect/L2 Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need udma_subsystem internal APB register decode, channel state machines, address/length update policy, address/range checks, and error/deny response handling. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-UDMA-RANGE-005.

findings.summary: Potential GPIO lock bypass: REG_PADOUTSET and REG_PADOUTCLR update r_gpio_out without checking r_gpio_lock[2], while direct REG_PADOUT is gated by r_gpio_lock[2]. However, security impact depends on whether these GPIO pins are designated sensitive.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure/expose / gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_in/gpio_in_sync, interrupt configuration, gpio_padcfg / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester reaching GPIO Operation: read/write/configure/expose Object: gpio_out, gpio_dir, gpio_in/gpio_in_sync, interrupt configuration, gpio_padcfg Expected guard: GPIO direction/output/interrupt control that can affect board-level security signals should be protected by pin policy, lock, or privilege guard where pins are designated sensitive. Observed behavior: apb_gpio accepts writes on PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. REG_PADDIR checks r_gpio_lock[0], REG_PADOUT checks r_gpio_lock[2], but REG_PADOUTSET and REG_PADOUTCLR update r_gpio_out by OR/AND with PWDATA without checking the output lock. REG_INTEN, REG_INTTYPE0/1, REG_GPIOEN, REG_GPIOLOCK, and PADCFG writes are also raw PWDATA updates in the provided snippet. Path: APB bus -> periph_bus_wrap GPIO range -> s_gpio_bus -> apb_gpio -> GPIO external pins/interrupt Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The missing impact slot remains unresolved: the observations do not identify top-level GPIO pin mapping to security-sensitive board signals, straps, debug, lifecycle, or secrets. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-GPIO-PINPOL-006.

findings.summary: soc_peripherals connects apb_adv_timer to s_adv_timer_bus, slow clock, ext_sig_i=s_gpio_sync, events_o=s_adv_timer_events, and timer_ch0_o..timer_ch3_o. It also connects soc_event_generator outputs to timer event inputs and instantiates apb_timer_unit with APB configuration and irq_lo_o/irq_hi_o ports. The apb_timer_unit facts show CFG/TIMER/CMP/START/RESET registers and enable/IRQ/event-related bits.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger / advanced timer events/channels and timer_unit irq_lo_o/irq_hi_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester and external timer event inputs Operation: write/configure/trigger Object: advanced timer events/channels and timer_unit irq_lo_o/irq_hi_o Expected guard: Timer/event generator configuration capable of triggering interrupts, events, or output waveforms should be restricted if used for privileged control, and event inputs should not override protected timer state without enable/policy checks. Observed behavior: soc_peripherals connects apb_adv_timer to s_adv_timer_bus, slow clock, ext_sig_i=s_gpio_sync, events_o=s_adv_timer_events, and timer_ch0_o..timer_ch3_o. It also connects soc_event_generator outputs to timer event inputs and instantiates apb_timer_unit with APB configuration and irq_lo_o/irq_hi_o ports. The apb_timer_unit facts show CFG/TIMER/CMP/START/RESET registers and enable/IRQ/event-related bits. Path: APB bus -> s_adv_timer_bus/apb_adv_timer or s_apb_timer_bus/apb_timer_unit -> event generator/IRQ/PWM/channel outputs Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The missing impact slot remains unresolved: observations do not prove timer outputs are privileged/security-critical, nor show complete guard behavior for timer reconfiguration while running or external event influence. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-TIMER-EVENT-007.

findings.summary: apb_clkdiv directly updates clock divider values and valid strobes on APB writes without guard or safe-state checks, but provided evidence does not establish the protected-domain impact of those dividers.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/reset / clk_div0/1/2, clk_div*_valid, and SoC-control reset/clock-domain controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB requester reaching clock/reset control registers Operation: write/configure/reset Object: clk_div0/1/2, clk_div*_valid, and SoC-control reset/clock-domain controls Expected guard: Clock divider, clock selection, and reset-generation control registers should require privilege/lifecycle guard or safe-state checks before changing clock/reset behavior for protected domains. Observed behavior: apb_clkdiv writes r_clkdiv0/1/2 and asserts clk_div0/1/2_valid solely under PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE and address case. apb_soc_ctrl separately exposes cluster reset/power/fetch controls with raw APB writes as covered by PERM-APB-SOCCTRL-001. Path: APB bus -> apb_clkdiv and apb_soc_ctrl -> clock/reset generator/control outputs Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The missing impact slot remains unresolved for clk_div0/1/2: downstream clock/reset generator use sites and affected protected domains were not provided. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-CLKRST-008.

findings.summary: The fact layer identifies pulp_interfaces definitions for bbmux, clkgate, MMU sequential-secure size, and BIST controls/results, and pulp_soc connects dft_test_mode_i to soc_interconnect_wrap.test_en_i. No observation identifies an APB/debug-writeable driver for these signals or demonstrates missing test/lifecycle gating.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/override/route/expose / bist_en/bist_pattern/result, bbmux_* selects, clkgate_* selects, mmu_sram_seqsec_size, mmu_scm_seqsec_size / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB/debug/test requester or configuration driver Operation: configure/override/route/expose Object: bist_en/bist_pattern/result, bbmux_* selects, clkgate_* selects, mmu_sram_seqsec_size, mmu_scm_seqsec_size Expected guard: Test/BIST, mux, clock-gate, and MMU size/control signals should be driven only by authorized configuration or lifecycle/test mode and should not be writable or controllable by untrusted APB/debug paths in normal operation. Observed behavior: The fact layer identifies pulp_interfaces definitions for bbmux, clkgate, MMU sequential-secure size, and BIST controls/results, and pulp_soc connects dft_test_mode_i to soc_interconnect_wrap.test_en_i. No observation identifies an APB/debug-writeable driver for these signals or demonstrates missing test/lifecycle gating. Path: pulp_interfaces test/BIST/MMU/clock-gate/bbmux interfaces -> SoC memory/core routing controls Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The missing impact slot remains unresolved, and no APB/debug reachability to these test/mux/MMU/clkgate drivers was shown. Need connected drivers/use sites and normal-vs-test-mode gating. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-TEST-MUX-009.

findings.summary: The SoC interconnect exposes multiple masters including FC, uDMA, debug/JTAG, and HWPE to shared memory/peripheral targets, but the visible interface connections and address-map facts do not show requester privilege/security attributes or requester-specific policy enforcement.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/fetch/route / mem_l2_bus, mem_l2_pri_bus, mem_rom_bus, apb_periph_bus, L2/SCM/ROM/APB target regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: FC data/instruction, debug/JTAG, uDMA, HWPE/cluster masters Operation: read/write/fetch/route Object: mem_l2_bus, mem_l2_pri_bus, mem_rom_bus, apb_periph_bus, L2/SCM/ROM/APB target regions Expected guard: Interconnect should preserve requester identity/security attributes or otherwise enforce address-range policy consistently for instruction/data/debug/DMA/JTAG masters accessing protected memory and peripheral regions. Observed behavior: pulp_soc connects lint_fc_data, lint_fc_instr, lint_udma_tx, lint_udma_rx, lint_debug, and lint_hwpe to soc_interconnect_wrap, which exposes APB, L2, private L2, and ROM target buses. The static/fact-layer interfaces show request/address/write/data style signals but no privilege/security metadata; the included address map defines target ranges but not requester-specific permissions. Path: pulp_soc master buses -> soc_interconnect_wrap/soc_interconnect -> ROM/L2/SCM memory buses and peripheral APB bridge Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Without requester-specific attributes or checks, debug/JTAG/uDMA/HWPE masters may be able to access the same memory/peripheral targets as FC instruction/data paths, causing potential read/write/fetch policy bypass for protected ROM/L2/SCM/APB regions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full decode/arbitration/default-error logic was truncated, so exact unauthorized requester-to-target path and denial behavior are not fully closed.

global_uncertainty: 5 evidence slot(s) remained incomplete after closure checking.
